info.name: Microsoft Azure SearchManagementClient
info.description: Client that can be used to manage Azure Cognitive Search services and API keys.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Get Providers Microsoft Search Operations

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for each request.

request.description: Lists all of the available REST API operations of the Microsoft.Search provider.

response.name: Success. The response describes the list of operations.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: (Required) The API version to use for each request.

response.name: Operations API failed.

(Required) The name of the Azure Cognitive Search service to create or update. Search service names must only contain lowercase letters, digits or dashes, cannot use dash as the first two or last one characters, cannot contain consecutive dashes, and must be between 2 and 60 characters in length. Search service names must be globally unique since they are part of the service URI (https://<name>.search.windows.net). You cannot change the service name after the service is created.

request.description: Creates or updates a search service in the given resource group. If the search service already exists, all properties will be updated with the given values.

response.name: The existing service definition was successfully updated. If you changed the number of replicas or partitions, the scale operation will happen asynchronously. You can periodically get your service definition and monitor progress via the provisioningState

request.description: Checks whether or not the given search service name is available for use. Search service names must be globally unique since they are part of the service URI (https://.search.windows.net).

response.name: The name check completed. The response contains details of whether the name is valid and available. If the name is invalid, the response also contains a message explaining why not.
